query: I'm allergic to nuts. Quick Thai-style noodles?

queries: Here is a quick and easy Thai-style noodle recipe for a weeknight dinner:
This fast Thai drunken noodles recipe comes together in 30 minutes:
Here is a simple Pad Thai recipe you can make at home quickly:

recommendation.headline: Drunken noodles are your best bet tonight — a fast, naturally nut-free Thai stir-fry that comes together in about half an hour.
recommendation.why_it_fits: You want quick and Thai-style without any nut worries, and drunken noodles fit perfectly: the Tastes Better From Scratch page is peanut-free start to finish and uses sesame or canola oil, so there's nothing to swap out. It's a one-pan stir-fry ready in around 30 minutes. Unlike pad Thai, which usually leans on peanuts, this dish keeps things naturally safe for your allergy.

missing_items.note: The backbone of the dish; regular rice noodles work in a pinch.
Regular basil is a fine stand-in if you can't find it.
Swap in tofu, shrimp, or extra veg if you'd rather.

primary_sources.title: Drunken Noodles (Pad Kee Mao) | - Tastes Better From Scratch

recommendation.how_to_use_sources: Follow the Tastes Better From Scratch Drunken Noodles page for the full method and sauce — it's already nut-free, so just cook it as written with sesame or canola oil. If you want a punchier, more takeout-style sauce, glance at the Half Baked Harvest drunken noodles page for its fish sauce and honey balance and borrow that idea.

recipe.title: Easy Drunken Noodles Recipe (Pad Kee Mao, 35 Minutes)

alternatives.reason: Reach for the Simply Recipes easy drunken noodles if you want a slightly more streamlined 35-minute version.

recipe.title: Better Than Takeout Thai Drunken Noodles. - Half Baked Harvest

alternatives.reason: The Half Baked Harvest version is the pick when you want a bolder, sweeter takeout-style sauce.

recipe.title: Pad See Ew (Thai Stir Fried Noodles) - RecipeTin Eats

alternatives.reason: Go with the RecipeTin Eats Pad See Ew if you'd prefer a milder, non-spicy soy-based stir-fry — just use the vegetable oil option.

candidates.title: Drunken Noodles (Pad Kee Mao) | - Tastes Better From Scratch

candidates.why_it_matches: A quick one-pan drunken noodle dish with sesame or olive oil and no nuts, safe for a nut allergy.

candidates.why_it_matches: Quick Thai stir-fried noodles with no peanuts; just use the vegetable oil option instead of peanut oil to keep it nut-safe.

candidates.why_it_matches: Nut-free spicy drunken noodles ready in under 30 minutes, though the write-up reads a bit machine-generated.

candidates.why_it_matches: Fast 35-minute drunken noodles with no nuts in the dish; choose the coconut oil option over peanut oil for the allergy.

candidates.why_it_matches: A fast 30-minute pad Thai, but peanuts are used as garnish so you'd need to omit them for a nut allergy.

candidates.why_it_matches: A quick pad Thai where peanuts are only an optional topping you can leave off, though the dish is designed around them.

candidates.title: Pad Thai
candidates.why_it_matches: Very fast pad Thai with no peanuts in the dish, but the recipe calls for peanut oil so swap in avocado or coconut oil for the allergy.

candidates.title: Easy Pad Thai Recipe (30 Minutes or Less!)
candidates.why_it_matches: Quick pad Thai but peanuts are stirred into the dish, so it would need adapting to be nut-safe.

candidates.why_it_matches: An authentic pad Thai with a peanut mixture worked into the dish plus a 30-minute noodle soak, so it's neither nut-safe nor fast.

query: Need an appetizer and a main for a date night dinner.

recommendation.dish_name: Seared Scallops to Start, Herb-Crusted Filet Mignon for the Main
recommendation.headline: Make it a proper night in: buttery seared scallops followed by an herb-crusted filet mignon, both from one plan built for two.
recommendation.why_it_fits: You asked for an appetizer and a main for date night, and the Perfect 3-Course Romantic Dinner page from easykitchenkey.com handles both in one go — scallops to open, filet mignon to anchor the table. It's elegant without needing three different sources, so you can focus on the cooking (and the company). Everything is scaled for an intimate dinner rather than a crowd.

missing_items.note: Get dry-packed if you can — they sear and brown far better than wet-packed.
Two thick-cut steaks; ask the butcher for center-cut for even cooking.
For the herb crust; regular breadcrumbs work in a pinch.
Dried herbs will do if you're not making a special trip.
Helps the crust adhere; a little mayo can stand in.

primary_sources.dish_name: Three-Course Romantic Dinner: Seared Scallops & Herb-Crusted Filet Mignon

recommendation.headline: Since your message came through a little scrambled, let's start with an easy weeknight win: 15-minute lemon pepper chicken.
recommendation.why_it_fits: Your request didn't give me much to go on, so I'm defaulting to something almost everyone can pull off with pantry staples. This one-pan lemon pepper chicken is fast, forgiving, and needs nothing exotic. If you had something else in mind, tell me what you're craving or what's in your fridge and I'll steer better.

missing_items.note: Thighs work just as well and stay juicier.
Fresh is best, but bottled juice will do in a pinch.
You can make your own with black pepper and lemon zest.
Olive oil is a fine swap.

recommendation.how_to_use_sources: Follow the Lemon Pepper Chicken page on natashaskitchen.com straight through for the pan method and timing. If you'd rather mix your own seasoning from scratch, the Lemon Pepper Chicken page on RecipeTin Eats shows how to build it from lemon zest and pepper.
